content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy explicitly states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" (privacy_policy text), but RunSessionManager.swift:1-16 imports CoreLocation, requests `requestWhenInUseAuthorization`/`startUpdatingLocation`, and stores a `route` of `CLLocation` objects, and the app description ("maps every run with GPS") confirms location is core functionality.
Pierre: This is a direct contradiction that Apple reviewers actively check — the app collects and stores GPS route data on-device while the privacy policy denies any location collection at all. Even if data stays local, the policy's blanket denial is factually false and must be corrected to describe the location usage, storage, and retention/deletion practices. Update the privacy policy text at runtracker.example/privacy to accurately reflect CoreLocation usage before resubmission.
